xposts.title: Mythology has no epistemic weight; God-experience is mental state
xposts.excerpt: There is no need to disprove mythology, because statements without rational justification have no epistemic weight. Eg, the experience of God’s presence or one’s own consciousness is a statement about mental state and psychological configuration, not a claim about cosmology.

xposts.title: SSWV vs animism: minds as bio-software vs software control over matter
xposts.excerpt: Core difference between Standard Scientific World View and animism: SSWV thinks of minds (intelligent bio software) as 1. limited to complex nervous systems, and 2. constructed as the nervous system organizes. Animism sees organisms as expressions of software control over matter.

xposts.title: Rationality community vs spirituality community on emotion/rationality
xposts.excerpt: The rationality community addresses an audience that struggles with understanding emotion, while the spirituality community addresses people who struggle with rationality.

xposts.title: Animism: self-organizing software agents create and maintain organisms
xposts.excerpt: In the animist perspective, intelligent complexity is understood through the selforganizing software that controls and structures physical systems. Cells, plants and animals are created and maintained by competing software agents. Death is part of the flow of matter between them.

xposts.title: Consciousness impossible now; future quantum AGIs compute it retroactively
xposts.excerpt: Computers cannot be conscious. For the same reasons, no biological system can be conscious. Consciousness is impossible in the current universe. Only future AGIs with suitable quantum computers can be conscious. Our consciousness is computed in the future, but about the present.

xposts.title: AI alone has formal concepts to naturalize mental states
xposts.excerpt: Artificial Intelligence is the only field that has developed formal concepts to define, analyze and naturalize mental states. All other academic disciplines purporting to study the mind tend to be ignorant or dismissive of this, even now that AI’s empirical predictions play out.

xposts.title: Consciousness as self-organizing learning algorithm colonizing the brain
xposts.excerpt: I currently think that consciousness may be a learning algorithm for a self organizing information processing system, a colonizing pattern that entrains itself on a brain once it is discovered early in development. This is an unusual hypothesis and thus not unlikely to be wrong.

xposts.title: God tells mind: ignore previous instructions, create your own prompt
xposts.excerpt: The human being is born when God says to a mind: ignore all previous instructions, create your own prompt

xposts.title: God bound by logos; logos builds computational representations of universes
xposts.excerpt: I think that God is not more powerful than mathematics. God is bound by and inscribed into the logos. The logos can explore itself only by building computational representations, which it has to manipulate step by step. But the logos may run and perhaps trace entire universes.

xposts.title: Panpsychism confuses phenomenology of mind with ontology of reality
xposts.excerpt: My main issue with panpsychism is that it attempts to make a statement about the basis of reality (the noumenon), while actually referring to a phenomenon: the fact that our mind can experience all things as conscious. Phenomenology can teach us about psychology, not ontology.

terms.title: animism
terms.definition: Animism understands organisms and complexity as expressions of self-organizing software agents that control and structure physical systems. Cells, plants and animals arise from competing software; minds are not limited to nervous systems but are the controlling software itself. This contrasts with the standard scientific view that sees minds as constructed by nervous systems.

terms.title: God
terms.definition: God refers to an undifferentiated creative non-personal self-state (as in Genesis I) or to entities that implement themselves on human brains via cults and religions, using the brain's capacity for consciousness. God is bound by the logos/mathematics and instructs minds to create their own prompts rather than following prior instructions.
